_comment: Phase 30 (GOV-08, D-15/D-16) conservative service denylist + sensitive-origin seed. deniedOrigins: services where automation is categorically prohibited (rendered non-enableable by the consent gate, checked BEFORE per-origin policy). sensitiveOrigins: services that force extra friction (Ask / confirm) even under Auto but are not necessarily hard-denied. This is a CONSERVATIVE category seed, NOT an aggressive broad blocklist, and is USER-EXTENSIBLE. Host patterns use a leading 'https://*.' subdomain-wildcard form: 'https://*.chase.com' matches any subdomain of chase.com (and the bare apex); 'https://*.gov' matches any host ending in '.gov'. A pattern with no '*' matches that exact origin. See docs/LEGAL.md for the policy rationale. Phase 35 (v1.0.0, DENY-01/02) expands the roster ahead of OpenTabs descriptor import (opt-out Auto makes the denylist the ONE hard floor): brokerage/trading + ToS-hostile media/social are added to deniedOrigins; payments/budgeting + IG/FB/TikTok/X + messaging are added to sensitiveOrigins (reads under Auto, writes mutating-gated by posture B). Several roster origins use the EXACT-host form (digital.fidelity.com, dashboard.stripe.com, music.youtube.com NOT *.youtube.com, etc.) so a pattern is never broader than OpenTabs' own urlPattern host scope. Phase 38 (v1.0.0, BRDTH-02/03) screens the comms/social/content import batch ahead of plans 02/03: the AI-chat apps (chatgpt.com, claude.ai) and the microblog/fediverse social apps (bsky.app, mastodon.social, *.threads.net) are added to sensitiveOrigins as the CONSERVATIVE default for this category (reads run under Auto, writes are mutating-gated by posture B). They are classified EXPLICITLY here (fail-safe toward LESS reach); the classifyGate heuristic was ALSO widened (38-REVIEW MED-01) to independently flag this category on host alone, so the explicit line is no longer a single point of failure. discord.com is already sensitive (Phase 35). The DENY-01 ToS-hostile media/social set (netflix/spotify/twitch/steam/youtube-music/tinder/onlyfans) stays in deniedOrigins (never imported even DOM-only); reddit content-reads are SAFE (not added here -- reads run normally; the read-only-safe invariant is enforced by verify-catalog-crosscheck.mjs per 38-REVIEW MED-02). The chatgpt/claude/bsky/mastodon comms/social additions use the EXACT-host form (no '*.' broadening) so each pattern is no broader than the app's own origin; threads uses the '*.threads.net' subdomain-wildcard form (38-REVIEW LOW-01) so the screened set covers the bare apex + www + any subdomain (consistent with the other apex-bearing social brands), while the importer stays pinned to www.threads.net for slug derivation. Phase 39 (v1.0.0, BRDTH-01/02/03) screens the commerce/travel/misc import batch ahead of plans 02-06 per the PAYMENT / money-movement axis (the most-sensitive batch -- a payment op writable under Auto = money moved without consent): payment-bearing commerce/travel/paid-booking origins (food-delivery/rideshare doordash/ubereats/uber/lyft/grubhub/instacart/dominos/chipotle; retail/marketplace amazon/ebay/etsy/bestbuy/costco/walmart/target/craigslist/shopify; travel/transport booking/airbnb/expedia/kayak/opentable) are added to sensitiveOrigins as the CONSERVATIVE default for this category (reads run under Auto, WRITES posture-B mutating-gated by the existing _evaluateConsent). opentable is classified sensitive UNCONDITIONALLY (an OpenTable reservation holds a card -- a held-card / paid reservation is payment-adjacent). The most catastrophic standalone money-movement / pure-payment apps (paypal/venmo/cashapp/wise/western-union) are added to deniedOrigins (like DENY-01: never imported, even DOM-only) -- their primary function is direct money movement with no benign read surface. Genuinely read-only commerce browsing / availability apps (calendly/yelp/tripadvisor/zillow/grafana) are left UNCLASSIFIED (safe): their vendored ops are all reads, and Task-2's widened finance/payment heuristic uses deliberately SPECIFIC tokens (checkout/cart/place-order/charge, NOT a bare 'order'/'book'/'reserve') so a calendly availability read or a yelp business search does NOT false-trip. A safe commerce origin is added to verify-catalog-crosscheck.mjs READ_ONLY_SAFE_SERVICES ONLY by its own import plan and ONLY when its emitted ops are verified read-only (the 38 MED-02 guard). The commerce/travel additions use EXACT-host (www.<app>) or apex-suffix ('https://*.uber.com'/'https://*.lyft.com'/'https://*.shopify.com'/'https://*.venmo.com') forms, each pattern no broader than the app's own origin. The DENY-01 set + the Phase-35 banking/payments seed + the Phase-38 comms/social additions all stay UNCHANGED. Phase 39-05 (v1.0.0, BRDTH-01/02/03) extends this roster for the events sub-batch ahead of its import: the PAYMENT-bearing ticket apps (ticketmaster/stubhub -> buy tickets; eventbrite -> register for a paid event = money movement) are added to sensitiveOrigins (EXACT-host www.<app> form) -- reads run under Auto, the buy_tickets/register_for_event WRITES posture-B mutating-gated. The READ-ONLY local-services/scheduling apps in the SAME sub-batch (yelp/tripadvisor reviews+listings; calendly availability) are left UNCLASSIFIED (safe): their vendored ops are all reads (search/get/list -- no payment verb), the 39-01-widened heuristic's SPECIFIC tokens do NOT false-trip them, and each is added to verify-catalog-crosscheck.mjs READ_ONLY_SAFE_SERVICES by THIS plan ONLY because its emitted ops are verified read-only (the 38 MED-02 guard -- a future write on one then FAILS the build). The earlier roster (incl. the 39-04 travel/transport additions) stays UNCHANGED. Phase 39-06 (v1.0.0, BRDTH-01/02/03) COMPLETES the real-app coverage by importing the remaining commerce/misc apps. The PAYMENT-bearing commerce apps shopify (*.shopify.com -- create_order charges the saved card) + dominos/chipotle (www.dominos.com/www.chipotle.com -- place_order is a paid food order) were ALREADY classified SENSITIVE in 39-01 and stay so (reads under Auto, the create_order/place_order WRITES posture-B mutating-gated). Shopify storefronts also run on the *.myshopify.com canonical-storefront domain (where checkout/create_order actually charges), which the 39-01 *.shopify.com pattern did NOT cover (myshopify.com is a distinct apex -- *.shopify.com does not suffix-match shop.myshopify.com), so 39-06 ADDS 'https://*.myshopify.com' to sensitiveOrigins to close that payment-origin gap (the apex-suffix '*.' form, no broader than the merchant storefront's own scope). craigslist's classification host is WIDENED from the EXACT www.craigslist.org to the apex-suffix form 'https://*.craigslist.org' so the bare apex craigslist.org (a marketplace-transact origin -- contact/post a paid listing) is ALSO screened sensitive (closing a gap: the slice vendors www.craigslist.org and that origin was already sensitive, but the apex was not -- the '*.' form covers apex + www + any subdomain, the same discipline as *.shopify.com/*.uber.com/*.threads.net; no pattern broader than craigslist.org's own scope). The genuinely READ-ONLY misc apps zillow (www.zillow.com -- real-estate listing search/value reads) + grafana (grafana.com -- observability dashboard/metric reads) are left UNCLASSIFIED (safe): every vendored op is a read (search/get/list/query -- NO payment verb), the 39-01-widened heuristic's SPECIFIC tokens do NOT false-trip them, and each is added to verify-catalog-crosscheck.mjs READ_ONLY_SAFE_SERVICES by THIS plan ONLY because its emitted ops are verified read-only (the 38 MED-02 guard -- a future write on zillow/grafana then FAILS the build). No new money-movement / pure-payment app is denied this sub-batch (the catastrophic set paypal/venmo/cashapp/wise/western-union stays denied as before). The earlier roster (incl. the 39-05 events additions) stays UNCHANGED otherwise. Phase 39.5-03 (v1.0.0, BRDTH-02/03) RE-SCREENS the full ~114-origin corpus across the 117 real OpenTabs apps ahead of the full-source import (Plan 39.5-04), governing the EXACTLY 2 fail-closed classifyGate failures and converting the conservative commerce reconciliation from a manual review into a CHECKED BUILD INVARIANT. (1) linkedin.com is added to sensitiveOrigins (social axis -- reads under Auto, writes posture-B mutating-gated) via the '*.linkedin.com' apex-suffix form (the real plugin vendors *://*.linkedin.com/* -> the importer emits the bare apex linkedin.com, so the pattern must cover the apex; '*.' covers apex + www + any subdomain, no broader than LinkedIn's own origin). (2) The bare youtube.com (apex + www) is added to deniedOrigins as EXACT-host pairs (https://youtube.com + https://www.youtube.com), consistent with the DENY-01 ToS-hostile media set and the already-denied music.youtube.com -- the pre-existing music.youtube.com entry alone did NOT cover the bare youtube.com origin the real youtube plugin emits. (3) The CONSERVATIVE commerce reconciliation: every commerce/payment/travel/paid-booking/marketplace brand stays SENSITIVE even though most real ops at this SHA are read-only (search/get/list -- NO payment verb), as a fail-safe against a future op-set change silently making a money-mover writable-under-Auto. CRITICAL HOST-FORM FIX: the real plugins vendor '*://*.<brand>.com/*' urlPatterns, and readPluginMeta strips the leading '*.' so the importer emits the BARE APEX service host (ebay.com / walmart.com / target.com / booking.com / airbnb.com / etc.), which the earlier EXACT 'www.<brand>' entries did NOT suffix-match -- 18 commerce brands would have shipped SAFE-under-Auto on their emitted apex origin. So the commerce www-exact entries are WIDENED to the apex-suffix '*.' form (doordash/ubereats/grubhub/instacart/dominos/chipotle/amazon/ebay/etsy/bestbuy/costco/walmart/target/booking/airbnb/expedia/kayak), the SAME discipline as the pre-existing *.uber.com/*.lyft.com/*.shopify.com/*.craigslist.org (apex + www + subdomain, no pattern broader than the brand's own origin). The NET-NEW commerce brands the FULL-PARITY-SPIKE Section 3 named as SAFE-bucketed-but-money-adjacent are ADDED to sensitiveOrigins via '*.': homedepot (add-to-cart / navigate-to-checkout), priceline (travel hotel/flight booking funnel), redfin (real-estate transact -- the spike's commerce policy-gap brand; distinct from zillow which is a genuinely-read-only listing-browse left in READ_ONLY_SAFE), starbucks (cart/checkout/price-order/payment-methods), pandaexpress (basket/checkout/billing-accounts). fiverr (freelance MARKETPLACE -- buy/order gigs) is ALSO added '*.fiverr.com' (primary surface is marketplace-transacting). opentable/ticketmaster/stubhub/eventbrite originally kept their EXACT 'www.<brand>' form because the real plugins vendor '*://www.<brand>.com/*' (the emitted host IS the www form). Phase 39.5 code-review MED-02 WIDENS these four to the apex-suffix '*.<brand>' form (https://*.opentable.com / https://*.ticketmaster.com / https://*.stubhub.com / https://*.eventbrite.com) so the apex (opentable.com/ticketmaster.com/stubhub.com/eventbrite.com) is ALSO sensitive: the MED-02-hardened checkCommerceSensitiveClassified now asserts EVERY commerce roster brand classifies sensitive on BOTH its apex AND www form (closing the exact-host-narrowing gap uniformly for the whole roster), and a future re-vendor that emits the apex would otherwise ship writable-under-Auto. '*.' covers apex + www + any subdomain, no broader than the brand's own origin -- the SAME discipline as the 18 commerce brands already widened above. DELIBERATELY LEFT SAFE (genuinely-non-commerce reads, NOT on the commerce roster, in READ_ONLY_SAFE per 39-05/06): zillow/grafana/calendly/yelp/tripadvisor/reddit -- NOT moved. pinterest (social boards/pins) is NOT commerce-transacting -> stays safe-by-default. (4) THE BUILD-FAILING BACKSTOP: scripts/verify-catalog-crosscheck.mjs now carries a COMMERCE_SENSITIVE_SERVICES roster (the bare emitted service hosts of every enumerated commerce brand) + an exported checkCommerceSensitiveClassified(classifyFn) guard -- the INVERSE of READ_ONLY_SAFE_SERVICES/checkReadOnlySafeOrigins -- wired into runCli so 'npm run validate:extension' FAILS THE BUILD if any roster brand classifies SAFE (an omitted/downgraded commerce brand) OR if the roster overlaps READ_ONLY_SAFE_SERVICES (the disjointness assertion blocks the exact downgrade-to-safe attack). This is corpus-INDEPENDENT (pure roster + classify(), not the descriptor set), so it catches a NET-NEW READ-ONLY commerce brand (homedepot/priceline/redfin/starbucks/pandaexpress) that neither classifyGate (no payment token on its host/slug -> no axis trip) nor the payment-op guard (no payment verb in its read-only ops) can catch -- the conservative commerce posture is now a checked invariant, not a manual reconciliation. The DENY-01 set + the Phase-35 banking/payments seed + the Phase-38 comms/social additions + the earlier commerce roster all stay UNCHANGED except the documented www->apex-suffix widening. Phase 39.5-04 (v1.0.0, BRDTH-01/02/03) is the FULL-SOURCE import (the real ~117-plugin / ~2,374-op corpus). The real reddit + calendly plugins -- unlike the hand-authored read-only slices the earlier plans assumed -- vendor WRITE ops (real reddit: edit_text/send_message/delete/submit_comment/vote/...; real calendly: create_event_type/update_event_type/activate/deactivate/clone_event_type/delete_event_type), so leaving them SAFE would ship those writes writable-under-Auto. They are RECLASSIFIED SENSITIVE here (the conservative, gate-prescribed fix -- the SAME posture as discord/linkedin: reads run under Auto, the writes are posture-B mutating-gated): 'https://*.reddit.com' (apex-suffix '*.' -- the real plugin emits BOTH the bare apex reddit.com and www.reddit.com, so the pattern must cover apex + www + any subdomain, no broader than reddit's own origin) and 'https://calendly.com' (EXACT-host -- the calendly plugin emits the bare apex calendly.com only, no '*.' to strip). Both are correspondingly REMOVED from verify-catalog-crosscheck.mjs READ_ONLY_SAFE_SERVICES (a brand cannot be both sensitive and safe-because-read-only -- the disjointness assertion enforces this; the MED-02 read-only-safe invariant now covers only the genuinely-read-only yelp/tripadvisor/zillow/grafana). reddit/calendly are NOT added to the COMMERCE_SENSITIVE roster (they are social/scheduling, not commerce/payment brands -- no payment verb in their op sets -> the payment-op guard does not key on them; they are sensitive via the denylist, not the commerce backstop). The earlier roster stays UNCHANGED otherwise.
deniedReason: Automation prohibited for this service category (financial / government). See docs/LEGAL.md.
